headline: **MLB Daily Roundup: April 23, 2026**
article:
The St. Louis Cardinals fell to the Miami Marlins 4-1, with Iván Herrera leading the Cardinals' offense with a home run and one RBI. In other action, the Houston Astros secured a shutout victory over the Cleveland Guardians, with Peter Lambert pitching 6.0 innings and allowing 0 earned runs. The Tampa Bay Rays dominated the Cincinnati Reds 6-1, with Nick Martinez earning the win after pitching 8.0 innings.

Injuries continue to impact teams, with several players landing on the injured list. Francisco Lindor of the New York Mets was placed on the 10-day injured list due to a left calf strain. The Atlanta Braves also placed Dylan Dodd on the 15-day injured list retroactive to April 22, 2026, due to left thoracic spine inflammation.

Several trades and roster moves have taken place, including the Houston Astros trading RHP Wilmy Sanchez to the New York Yankees for SS Braden Shewmake. The Los Angeles Dodgers traded RHP Axel Perez to the Baltimore Orioles for RHP Chayce McDermott. The New York Mets traded LHP Richard Lovelady to the Washington Nationals for cash.

top_news.title: **Cardinals Fall to Marlins in Miami**
top_news.summary: The St. Louis Cardinals suffered a 4-1 loss to the Miami Marlins, with Iván Herrera leading the Cardinals' offense with a home run and one RBI. Michael Petersen impressed in relief for the Marlins, striking out three batters in a scoreless inning.

top_news.title: **Astros Shut Out Guardians 2-0**
top_news.summary: The Houston Astros secured a shutout victory over the Cleveland Guardians, with Peter Lambert pitching 6.0 innings and allowing 0 earned runs. Yordan Alvarez led the Astros' offense with a 3-hit, 2-RBI performance, including his 11th home run of the season.

top_news.title: **Reds Fall to Rays in Dominant Performance**
top_news.summary: The Tampa Bay Rays defeated the Cincinnati Reds 6-1, with Nick Martinez earning the win after pitching 8.0 innings, striking out 6 and allowing 1 earned run. Junior Caminero led the Reds' offense with a home run and 2 RBIs.

top_news.title: **Orioles Take Down Royals in Thrilling 8-6 Victory**
top_news.summary: The Baltimore Orioles secured an 8-6 win over the Kansas City Royals, with Coby Mayo leading the charge at the plate, hitting a home run and driving in three runs. Anthony Nunez earned the save in relief, striking out two batters in a scoreless inning.

top_news.title: **Braves Take Down Nationals 7-2**
top_news.summary: The Atlanta Braves secured a 7-2 victory over the Washington Nationals, led by strong performances from multiple players. Impact hitters for the Braves included Matt Olson, Ozzie Albies, Michael Harris II, and others.

top_news.title: **Tigers Take Down Brewers in Thrilling Finish**
top_news.summary: The Detroit Tigers edged out the Milwaukee Brewers 5-4 in a closely contested matchup. Impact hitters for the Tigers included Riley Greene and Spencer Torkelson, while Gary Sánchez and David Hamilton led the Brewers' offense.

top_news.title: **Lindor Lands on Injured List**
top_news.summary: New York Mets shortstop Francisco Lindor has been placed on the 10-day injured list due to a left calf strain.

top_news.title: **Astros Acquire Shewmake in Trade**
top_news.summary: The Houston Astros have made a move to bolster their roster, acquiring shortstop Braden Shewmake from the New York Yankees. The trade sent right-handed pitcher Wilmy Sanchez to the Yankees.
